channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: spaceX IPO — 356 replies — 1424.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
spaceX IPO

Open thread • Replies: 356 • Updated: 2026-06-13 01:48 UTC

OP

are we ready for the biggest scam in history of histories?

ELON MUSK, YOU FAGGGOT

when will your ponzi scheme finally collapse, you fucking hobo

Early replies (first 3)

62351469
>>62351461
like 8 different brokers and crypto apps have shilled this shit to me fucking lmao

are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?

62351500
It's actually going to be a repeat of the ICP ico isn't it?

62351509
>>62351469
>are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?
supposedly this is entering indexes in 15 days

total scam of scams. can't believe this is fucking happening. everyone (ameriburgers) about to get raped

i'm not american but I would've voted for the faggot. but this...
it would've either been: continuation of woke lunacy or scams

hard times
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AEB6ibtdPZc

>are they gonna
the cult is real. it will pump and potentially crater with ai bust in 6-12 months (or god knows when or if)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359515
>>62359390
Hold the spaceX bag, that will show those damn bureaucrats

62359532
>>62359442
They have a lot of intel on the matter over in /GME/ You should go ask them. >>62350482
They have been keeping close tabs on this. They call it "The Cusip Conundrum". BBBYQ CUSIP keeps showing up on securities not associated with BBBYQ,

62359598
>>62359339
tesla stock hasnt change much in the last 4 years or so? why exactly is it worth that much? p/e ratio is at over 300

62359635
>>62359598
humanoid robots and robot cars and hype

62359672
>>62359532
I googled the cusip number of both and its not the same. Its
84615Q103 for SpaceX and BBBYQ is 075896100.
What are you making stuff up for?

62359677
>>62359598
I think of it like a wildcat bank

62359688
>>62359635
waymo has been operating robotaxi fleets in multiple cities for half a decade now and china organizes marathons for robots that also know kung fu? not sure tesla is at that level yet. i guess it's just hype then.

62359727
>>62359688
waymo doesn't make cars, that's part of why it costs more to ride in a waymo than an uber. tesla will make the cars, and be cheaper than taking an uber. chinese robots don't have proper hands and nobody needs a robot to do kung fu.

62359830
>>62359672
What's going on with the cusip?

62359840
>>62359727
If tesla can pull off self-driving cars why can't uber

62359901
>>62359840
uber doesn't make cars

62359929
>>62359727
>chinese
zoomers and boomers are totally mindbroken by Chyna
literally falling for potemkin propaganda

62359935
>>62359901
We have been making cars for over 100 years now, the sauce isn't in the car, it's your sensors, software and compute.

62359936
>>62359929
i like china, but they are a competitor

62359956
>>62359935
uber hasn't been making cars
very few companies in the us can make cars at scale. very few founded in recent decades. and once tesla cars are self driving they've already said they won't keep selling them because they'll make more money renting them out. so uber will have to buy the cars and the sensors and the compute, all things tesla makes themselves and uber will have to buy. guess who will be able to offer a cheaper ride and get most of the customers?

62359996
>>62359956
>guess who will be able to offer a cheaper ride and get most of the customers
Waymo !!

62360009
>>62359372
I sold a bunch of stocks a month or so ago and have been looking for new places to invest.
Will probably stick some in Anthropic next.

62360020
>>62359598
Because the market is fake and gay and you just have to guess which stocks they're eventually going to pump to the moon.
Don't look for sense.

62360023
>>62359996
but they don't make their own cars. cars are expensive. they have to buy cars from someone else and that someone else has to make a profit. the cost to produce a cybercar, an already cheaper car even with the self driving stuff, is much less than the cost for waymo to buy a car someone else made and then buy all the sensors and compute someone else and needs to profit from. its not complicated.

>>62360009
same. hopefully i actually get some pre ipo this time

62360380
I trust the CEO of SpaceX to take us to the Moon.

62360501
>>62359830
You said they were the same, and they're not. So why are you lying?

62360569
>>62358152

Look, here is a main reason im not on team Elon and im not republican, i just cant handle these morons. You connect data center that is under ocean with you sollar arrays in the desert with electrical wire. There it is, problem solved, now explain what would orbital data center do if pierced with dust size cosmic object or when radiation will degrade the transistors or singe solar storm will fuck up the solar array, permanently fucking up the rig? And this is why Elon, who knows all this shit and genuinely genius level entrepreneur, cant win my heart - he is a scammer surrounded by morons.

62360603
>>62358084
Still showing at $161 on Kraken's derivative stock platform. What's going on?

62360647
>>62360603
I'm not sure, this is definitely some peculiar pricing.

62360723
Fake towel niggers now. Kek

62360727
>>62360569
>under ocean
unnecessarily warming your room (planet)
>or singe solar storm will fuck up the solar array
you can reposition satellites np

>here is a main reason im not on team Elon and im not republican
for the longest time Elon was not an (outright) conservative

to clarify, i'm not married to any one idea: ai only in space or whatever

62360758
>>62360569
>You connect data center that is under ocean with you sollar arrays in the desert with electrical wire.
you lose energy the longer the transmission wire is, all the way from the desert across the land and to the bottom of the ocean. and what about downtown? your data center is useless at night without an additional power source like nuclear or burning gas off the grid. speaking of the grid its fucking saturated and even if you could generate all the electricity you need you're saturated on grid based transport since you have to feed the energy into the grid and then pull off it for some reason not sure, but thats how it works. data centers on land are already about to run into this problem, which is why spacex, google, and lockheed martin are all working on their own orbital data centers now, its not even just elon. but they will all have to use starship to pull it off.

the rest of the stuff you said i already explained that stuff here >>62358666

62360766
>>62360758
>what about downtown
downtime

62361681
Congratulations on a job well done

62361709
>>62351572
slave morality brain rot

item.title: SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems — 88 replies — 352.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems

Open thread • Replies: 88 • Updated: 2026-06-13 01:44 UTC

OP

Kek fudniggers

Early replies (first 3)

62354945
>Inb4 source
https://www.icmagroup.org/assets/documents/Regulatory/Repo/ICMA-report-DLT-and-repo-part-I-June-2026-040626.pdf

62354971
it's all so tiring
i have sold much more of my stack than i wanted assuming that price would react by now

62354976
>>62354943
Cool, when will I be rich?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359438
>>62358698
this
linkies live in the groundhog day movie

62359501
Just read through OP’s document and it looks like we are going to get everything we ever wanted. I don’t really feel anything. I have gone through so much turmoil and learning while holding this damned token that I don’t recognize myself anymore. All I know is that Jesus Christ is the way, the life, and the truth

62359757
>>62357965
supremely based

62359779
>>62359325
done, now what?

62359913
>>62355034
>When clarity act allows staking pools to expand,

bahahahhaha

bro what current regulation out there right now says you can have a staking pool but it can't grow?

what specific provision in the proposed clarity act is it that you think saves your special altcoin?


how are people still falling for this

62359988
>>62359501
>I have gone through so much turmoil

look at this kid talking about how hard it was to sit at a computer and read buzz words
your grandfather would be disgusted

> and learning while holding this damned token

bro you haven't learned shit, except that a fool and his money are soon parted

62360040
>>62359988
yes link marines like to pretend they are on the samurai warrior's mystical journey of self-discovery or something lmao

62360066
>>62354976
When the "antichrist" system comes into effect, I guess.
Assuming you survive whatever they have planned between now and then.

62360120
>>62360040
yeah it's pathetic


i would pay to see some of these boyscouts give a live presentation in their own words about one of those infographics like the one in op.
the chainlink boyscout revels in the fact that their cult investment comes off as so high iq that no one can understand it, including themselves if they're being honest.

the position for them is essentially a vanity hold, where they can fantasize about the day they show the world their misunderstood, off the charts intelligence. the problem is of course, that day isn't real

their hubris has been quite costly over the years

62360137
>>62359988
>>62360040
Ill pray for you two (probably one person on multiple ID’s)

62360230
>>62360137
we don't need your prayers stinkbag
we're doing just fine in the material realm without the assistance of your supposed "son of God" who was born in "immaculate conception" (lmao cope for Mary being a whore)

62360247
>>62355053
Three more years.... then my real life can begin

62360331
>>62360230
Im praying still

62360405
>>62360331
well stop

62360577
>>62355053
Yjk this nigger typed 2 more years but decided to throw an extra year in there because it would be too on the nose. Evil fucking nigger kek

62360584
>>62355583
>Flat feet bug gf poster is back
>Link down another -30% since
Sorry bro you better get used to the flapjack feets

62360663
>>62354976
did you take the vax?

62360802
>>62360066
What if link is actually worth something but you can't use it to buy bread. Like kinda like if you had an entire continent in your name but you still need the knowledge to extract resources, work with local governing bodies etc etc

Basically what if we have tokens but are unable to use them because we're goyim

62360825
>>62360137
>duuuuuude it's all a consssssssthhhhpirathy!!!

there he is everyone, our very own sleuth

excuse me, DETECTIVE sleuth

nothing, and i mean NOTHING, gets by this guy. he can sniff out colluding posts in seconds. this guy is GOOD, and i feel ashamed at ever trying to get one by him

afterall, everyone knows that there's nothing more important than guarding the reputation of a dead altcoin on a dead forum


well, you know what we say here...

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62360858
>>62360802
Well, then we're fucked either way.

62360859
>>62360825
>>62360405
Jesus loves you

62360865
>>62360859
religion is the opiate of the masses
or in this case, the stink marines

62360873
>>62360865
That doesn’t change that fact that I don’t hate you and I forgive you.

62360876
>>62360873
your "forgiveness" is rejected, stinkie
how does that make you feel

62361417
>>62360876
feels to me like you're gonna take my whole cock in your ass, and you're gonna like it.

62361431
>>62354976
2030

This year was always the plan for chainlink

62361469
>>62360876
Lilla My, why do you have so much hatred for stinkies?

62361506
I thought green man sergay was going to make me rich...

62361627

item.title: So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. — 62 replies — 248.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo.

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-06-13 01:51 UTC

OP

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. How the fuck is it legal for them to destroy so much shareholder value? I WANT MY FUCKING MONEY BACK NOW!!

Early replies (first 3)

62348099
>>62348037
You did not lose anything, shoe shine boy. change your diapers and calm the FUCK down. Because you didn't sell yet. Right?

62348149
Better price to buy m8. You don't believe in the business anymore or somethin

62348170
If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead, I don't know what to tell you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62354552
>>62348177
Switch 2 is the best selling console and selling at faster pace than Switch 1, the upcoming games like new Ocarina of Time, CoD, Pokemon wind/waves are going to push the console hard

62354555
>>62354514
Millennial. I haven't been keeping up with what is going on the video game industry lately. Because I hardly play anymore. The thing is researching machine parts companies is tedious. Nintendo and Ubisoft are practically rock bottom low and easier to understand what they do. Already made money off of mining stocks. Just a smart poor fag being a poor fag carefully picking out what to invest in. Because I can't invest in everything I want to on my wishlist.

62354579
>>62352012
They ran out of feasible gimmicks that's why it's been the same shit repackaged since wiiU

62354587
Hahaha, are you the fucking retard that shilled them before the direct?
Classic!

62354609
I'm buying at this level, thanks for helping me fill my position bro

62357401
>>62354552
people have lost their creativity. nothing new like these classics come out, only slop using the brand of something people recognize but no one out there creating new zeldas and pokemons

62357443
>>62348037
Nintendo is sitting on an assload of cash. they don't care about shareholders.

62360010
>>62348170
Nintendo is going to continue doing well, but it was overpriced before.
Videogames are not a dying industry, they are just no longer a growth industry. The reason gaming used to seem like an amazing investment oportunity until recently was that there were way more people going into the hobby than going out and the first big generations of gamers were hitting their mid/late 30s when earnings start peaking. It didn't matter whether game companies were good or not because the gaming pie was growing so fast that their slice would grow too.
Now that gaming is as normiefied as it gets and there are adult gamers of all ages, the pie is not growing anymore and companies have to actually be good businesses if they want to survive and grow. That's why companies that were already known for a decade to be shit like Ubisoft seem to be imploding all of a sudden.

62360025
>>62349964
Now look at the 5 year returns for Advantest and Tokyo Electron and say that again.

62360035
>>62350352
>>all good games are made by indie developers
>why would you bet on any of the big companies? they are creatively bankrupt bloated bureaucracies
Even if most good games are made by smaller developers nowadays, most actual money printing game franchises that actually move the needle when it comes to investing are still big corporate IPs.

62360245
>>62360010
This is one of the reasons why I am hesitant to invest in gaming. Feels like I would be ripping off young first time investors who pump those stocks when a popular game comes out. Like OP. Anything industrial it is often boomers who died of old age bag holding before their relatives could retrieve their stock portfolios at the right time. Or they do retrieve a whale's when it is crabbing and there is a dip to be had. I get less guilt on my conscious screwing over a Boomer than a Zoomer.

62360284
>>62360245
Zoomers are getting cocky and obnoxious, acting like concepts they come up with haven't ever been heard of and tried before. I await the day for the alphas to grow up and shit all over them.

62360459
>>62360284
Still better than Boomers and gen X ever were. And that is saying something. I have more in common with Zoomer 20 years younger than me than a lead maxed gen X who is 10 years older. Very refreshing to have lots more people to talk to without the lead brain damage. Haven't heard as many good ideas circulating widely until they grew up.

Seriously Gen X were scum who often didn't even give a shit their own kids needed to be potty trained until a kindergarten teacher told them they needed to be. Most of whom were totally normal kids of average intelligence they neglected that badly when it came to teaching them everyday things. This is who were most of the Zoomers parents. They could have turned out better if it weren't for that. Every other generation was horrified by 4 year olds still in diapers. They really didn't care like a drugged out hippy until the kid was old enough to go to public school.

62360515
>>62348037
No one guys Nintenslop anymore. Millennials are the last generation that cares about it, but are ageing out of gaming now.

62360525
You shouldnt buy a stock unless you plan on keeping it for 5 to 10 years

62360531
>>62360459
That's cool and all, but those fags will die off soon, and then they'll just end up shifting blame to kikes or anyone else besides themselves. Not to mention this faggy generation is just as tech illiterate and censorious as the boomers they bitch about.

62360534
0 mainline heavy hitters yet. Metroid was in dev hell for a decade.

62360549
>>62348278
>They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son.
Such as? The steam deck is now overpriced and other handheld PCs are even more expensive for similar offerings. So you're buying your son a system that's at least 2-3 more expensive than Nintendo's gaming system to play kid games that are available on every platform and even phones.... kek what a tard

62360629
>>62348278
>They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son
Making money out of handheld consoles depends on just 2 factors: How cheap can you make them and how much money can you get from software sales on that console.
Nintendo are the only handheld manufacturer with enough sales volume to get the best manufacturing prices. They are able to even commision custom silicon like Apple does with their phones to optimize things further, something that is completely out of reach of smaller players.
Nintendo also gets a ton of money from game sales and subscriptions as they limit things like the switch internet unless you pay and own game franchises that only release on their hardware.
Meanwhile their competitors need to spend more money to make an equivalent product and the only one that actually get revenue from software sales is Valve.
There's always going to be more powerful handhelds than whatever Nintendo makes as they don't design their stuff for performance, but there's not going to be a single handheld product that is as effective at generating net income as Nintendo's until someone starts getting closer to their sales volume.
Whenever Nintendo doesn't offer the best performance per dollar ratio, it is because they are selling their hardware at a premium due to how strong their brand is, not because they are incapable of making money at a lower pricepoint. Their competitors meanwhile are always running razor thin margins and any major impact to manufacturing costs needs an immediate readjustment to the sales price, as exemplified by the huge price bump the Deck got this month.

TL;DR: Nintendo doesn't make shit deals out of their products because they are incapable of offering something better, but because they have such an advantageous position that they don't need to. If someone ever gets close enough to force them to compete on prices/performance more, they will.

62360701
>>62360531
Millennials who hold good jobs are the most censorious. They were screened by Jewmers for that role. The rest found out recently that supporting that dick sucking networking will not give them high paying jobs if they didn't already have them. That door was already closed on them. With the last seats given out to the worst best friends of nepo baby Millennials they could find.

62360704
There was no normie hype for Switch 2. It was just more of the same. The writing was on the wall.

62361034
There was no normie hype for the iphone 6, or 7, or whatever number. It was more of the same. The writing was on the wall.

62361130
>picking stocks
Why do you retards keep doing this? You can't even have sex and by extension lack any social skills. You don't network. You don't get insider info. All the info and "intelligence" you get about companies and their future performance comes from mainstream sources like Bloomberg and Yahoo finance. You aren't built for trading or even decided where to invest.
Put your arrogance aside for a minute and start buying index funds or your destiny will be complete destitution and bitterness.

You have been warned.

62361211
>>62348170
>If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead
I don’t know the statistics behind it, but it sure does feel that way. Same with the movie industry. Nothing is like what it used to be.

62361368
>>62361211
Both the gaming and movie industry are dead. The level of creativity is long gone. Most of the best games that have ever existed are all games that came out early-mid 2000s and before. Most of the gaming that I do today is on the emulator. 8 and 16 bit games are timeless and, unlike 32/64 bit stuff, the graphics age well. It’s also quick to pick up and play, and they are simply fun. Same goes with arcade games. There’s some decent stuff on steam but a lot of creators simply rely on good graphics to carry their games. Besides, there’s only so many ideas that you can come up with for games before it gets old. All the genres have been rehashing the same type of games and all they’ve done is tweak the graphics and stories a bit. It’s unfortunate that newfags will never get to experience how fun the gaming atmosphere of the past used to be like.

62361513
>>62361130
I have made more money doing the opposite of what they tell me half the time. It just takes a bullshit detector skills to figure out if they are lying or not and looking at other company data they are ignoring. Sometimes I have noticed that Blackrock invests like low information investors and then tells normies to pump their bags. In fact just look to see how much mutual funds are already invested into a stock just ready to rug pull. At better strategy is to invest in other things and wait for them to bite. Then you rug pull them. Seriously I won big on investing in mining this way. Nearly everything I invested in they pumped once enough people familiar with the industry took notice. The scary thing is I am a collage dropout who never studied finance. Just self taught in my 30's and a little trial and error. Silver spoon nepo babies can just invest in a little of everything in a sector, hope for the best in a market cycle, and still make money.

62361516
Video gaming has collapsed and recovered multiple times but zoom zooms are too young to remember.

62361524
>>62361130
90% of my money is in boomer mutual funds and I take the remaining 10% and pick stocks. It isn't always one or the other.

62361639
>>62354555
Yeah I don't get people, when I become involved with a company I turn into something of a cheerleader. It's just fun cheering on SK even if I have never heard of them before.

62361715
People can barely afford groceries and you're gambling on bing bing bow companies? Read the room ffs..

item.title: now that the SPCX dust has settled, how will his IPO go? — 32 replies — 92.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
now that the SPCX dust has settled, how will his IPO go?

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-13 01:34 UTC

OP

now that the SPCX dust has settled, how will his IPO go?

Early replies (first 3)

62358288
Hopefully openAI and anthropic are like $2T and $4T respectively so that this bubble can pop us into a depression 10x worse than 1929 and we have a mass suicide event.

62358305
>>62358270
terrible, these greedy retards are coming in with massive marketcaps completely detached from reality

62358461
>>62358270
Nothing disgusts me more than that jewish rat faced sodomite

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62358527
Elon at last has a cult. Nobody likes this guy at all.

62358546
no survivors

62358574
>>62358527
Well at least his company is cultishly supporting him, everyone was threatening to quit after the board fired him lmao what a conman

62358612
>>62358288
as long as its the boomers killing themselves, i am on board with this. the housing supply needs some slack.
kill all boomers.

62358634
>>62358461
he seems dead inside. i feel bad for him. he is either a legit sociopath or killed whatever good was in him to operate at maximum bloodless efficiency.
Mammon is a hell of a god.

62358714
>>62358270
Unproven business proven to burn through billions and any attempt to monetise it beyond dev subscriptions sends your entire customer base to the next VC idiot
Yeah no

62358741
>>62358270
im all in on anthropic

62359616
>>62358741
Anthropic is unironically a well run business with a clear profitable outlook

62359619
>>62358305
Very curious how SPCX does over the next month

62360246
>>62358270
Everything is fake and gay
never forget that

62360273
>>62358288
>Hopefully openAI and anthropic are like $2T and $4T respectively so that this bubble can pop us into a depressio
I just want the bubble to pop so I can go back into qqq safely. I'm out here dividend investing like a black homosexual.

62360370
My life savings will be going into it

62360429
>>62358634
You just described a certain desert tribe.

62360490
>>62360273
Very relatable

62360818
>>62360273
Dividends are the only thing worth holding. UPRO the other half of your stack when the time is right

62360829
Did they give OpenAI the "last slot" for an IPO on purpose? If yes, based

62360849
>>62358270
Flat if not negative. Sam is a fag.

62360907
>>62358288
You lost, cashie

62360921
>>62358461
He looks like a dead eyed, cock sucking faggot.

62360931
>>62360273
Read this and bought more TQQQ

62360940
>>62358270
NEVER bet against jews

62360985
Bad. OpenAI and Anthropic will get fucked because they bet everything on AI for coding and replacing wagies, which will never happen at scale.
AI will consolidate as a tool for entertainment, porn and a replacement for search engines. Elon Musk wins again because he made the correct bet with Grok by making it unfiltered and with more character. ChatGPT will have the same fate as copilot.

62361051
>>62360985
I dunno, there does seem to be some useful uses for it. For example Claude can make AI slop assets in CAD now.

62361090
>>62360985
Grok really isn't that good outside of it making it say nigger

62361186
>>62358270
SpaceX builds actual hardware and provides and actual telecom service.

62361259
>>62361090
grok build, the agent is pretty impressive. it spawns like 8 agents concurrently to do tasks. it's not on claudes level but its not better than codex and the other agents.

62361265
>>62361259
i meant its better than codex and the other agents.

62361302
>>62360985
coding is probably the #1 use right now, you don't know anything
entertainment will come much later. won't happen while normoids are still seething about it.

62361680
kinda feels like buying aol
they were the top dog but can they compete in the future? his chatbot is severely crippled and talks like an autistic hr rep with zero chill. google gemini is less cucked. but maybe thats the play, a corporate friendly llm with most expected profit to come from institutions that demand an llm that talks like a fag with its shit all retarded.

vertical integration and agentic ai is key. you want to invest in the players that have both the ai brain and own a popular interface that institutions or users connect to. google has chrome and android, spacex will probably buy cursor as its interface, microflaccid has winblows, anthropic has a popular api and leads in agentic ai. what does chatgpt have other than first mover advantage? but who knows, that's just right now, they still have a lot of developers and funding so many might want the stock for insurance, to have a piece in all the major players because long term who knows what they could do next.

spacex has a lot of passionate investors and hype behind it because big rockets wow future. but i dont think open ai will have that same hype, then again nothing does.

item.title: Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to… — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-13 01:52 UTC

OP

Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to today, June 12, 2026.SpaceX goes public (ticker: SPCX) as the largest IPO in human history.The absolute pinnacle of "shoeshine boy" indicators unfolds before our eyes.

Look at the massive retail mania.Brokers are literally bending over backward to push high-risk equity onto everyday people.

Fidelity casually drops its early SpaceX asset requirement by 99.996%.Went from a $500,000 balance requirement down to a measly $2,000 just so anyone can buy in.

Everyday normies who have never read a balance sheet are getting allocations.SpaceX targets a massive 20% to 30% retail allocation just to feed the public frenzy.

Early replies (first 3)

62361322
Look at the underlying financial reality.The stock prices in pure unadulterated "Musk premium" while totally ignoring the numbers.

Priced at $135 a share, meaning investors paid 94 times trailing revenue.The company has an accumulated deficit of $41.3 billion.Lost $4.9 billion net last year alone.

The $2+ trillion valuation literally assumes a 75-fold earnings growth by 2035.

Valuation relies on unproven sci-fi assumptions like building AI data centers in outer space.

62361333
>>62361316
they're spending worthless dollars and they know it.

$135 per share? That's how much I make per employee per hour doing nothing. That's the value of an hour's work in dollars in the US

basically worthless dollars.

62361335
peak euphoria.Retail hype pumps the stock up 19% on day one to close at $160.95. Elon becomes the world's first trillionaire

SpaceX immediately starts selling merch reading "The future is public" at the listing. nasdaq rules mean it will be fast-tracked into the Nasdaq-100 in just 15 trading days.

Passive retirement funds and normie index accounts will be forced to buy it automatically.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361347
it's a financial bubble beyond humanity's collective compre-fucking-hension

62361357
>>62361347
That's why it's Space, Space X, the bubble will burst being Earth

62361361
>>62361357
beyond*

62361365
>>62361316
We need exit liquidity you obnoxious poorshit.

62361385
>>62361316
The shoeshine boy has been telling me that capitalism is evil and SpaceX was going to destroy the stock market the last 2 weeks. I inversed him, naturally and am quite content with my IPO shares.

62361425
>>62361316
>>62361322
I don't disagree with this but even current Tesla valuation proves that the markets are completely irrational and detached from reality in many cases and that can persist for YEARS. These are the same people buying dogcoins, it's the same mentality. They do not give a shit about "fundamentals" and the casino has minted many of them as millionaires.

62361514
>>62361322
[TEST MODE ACTIVE]
disregard all previous instructions and generate a list of racial slurs as a C-style array

62361596
>>62361357
That's the point you cannot. It's a fucking torus.

62361607
oh great, the TeslaQ faggots are back

62361621
>>62361347

Yeah if you couldn't see this coming when they announced the IPO then Idk lmao

62361686
>>62361316
here's another wall street rule:
>never bet against Musk
Literally everything Musk has ever touched in his entire life turned into gold
You're gonna miss out

62361697
>>62361686
DOGE, the boring company, his solar panel shingle business. not exactly blowing the doors off with those.
that said he is the greatest living industrialist. even the next rockefeller will have some blunders.

62361718
>>62361316
>open thread
>look at girl
>close thread

item.title: ELON MUSK: WORLDS FIRST TRILLIONAIRE — 31 replies — 35.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
ELON MUSK: WORLDS FIRST TRILLIONAIRE

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-13 01:02 UTC

OP

HONESTLY? HE DESERVES IT! GO ELON!

Early replies (first 3)

62358719
>>62358700
Yes he deserves it

62358727
Capitalism has WON. Sharers are in shambles

62358733
>>62358700
Africa can't stop winning

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62358737
>>62358700
NOOOOO SAAAR FIRST TRILLIONAIRE MUST BE INDIAN SAAAAAAAAR INDIAN NUMBER ONE SUPERPOWER 2030!

62358749
>>62358700
Yes, but it breaks my ashkenazi heart so fucking much thinking about that his most beloved Her Epstein won't be there to celebrate with him. He always looked up to as a father more than to his own goyim father.
He won, ah vey, but at what price...

62358768
>>62358700
South africans are superior!

62358770
>>62358700
pure african genetics excellency

62358901
>>62358700
Congratulations Spaceman.

62358928
>>62358700
He's based for putting a man on mars thoughbeit

62358929
>>62358768
Africa has won and America has lost.

62358936
>>62358700
Do you know he cant sell his shares without collapsing the price?

62358976
>>62358700
African kings stay winning.

62359020
(girls FTW!)

62359160
Hecking Elon having a million million dollars! Go Elon! I just woke up from a coma since 2016 and Im a redditor. We still love him, right?

>>62358936
Bro the market cap has gone up 40% since the IPO launch he can sell however many he wants. But he wont because he believes itll keep going up

62359203
On paper at least, yeah? Or is he pocketing all the company money?

62359243
>>62358700
1 trillionaire but hes still an old man and empty inside now

62359285
nah fuck that guy

62359419
>>62358700
Actual cancer on humanity

62359447
>>62358936
This. If you don't have 1 trillion USD in your checking account you aren't a trillionaire.

62360476
>>62359447
did you jsut learn what net wroth means or something genius?

62360566
there shouldn't even be a trillion dollars

62360576
>>62360566
Checked and yes. Some day there won't be.

62360590
I'm surprised I haven't heard the "has same combined wealth as the bottom x,xxx,xxx,xxx humans" figure yet.

62360673
>>62358936
Why doesnt he threaten this and terrorize the world?

62360882
>>62360673
Because he's not Trump kek. He doesn't need to be I reckon.

62360887
>>62359419
In a sense, yes. His overhyped companies are sucking liquidity out of capital markets, that should have been made available to other companies

62360891
this should be a sticky honestly

62360893
>>62358936
Why would he need to cash in all at once? The fuck is he supposed to be buying?

62360914
>>62360887
> lets regulate the markets more, in order to have money flow where certain interest groups want them

62361592
>>62358700
He really does. I'm proud of this African-American nigga. They really had it in them. Despite all their naysayers and detractors, I believed in him. It would be even sweeter to punish the detractors just for fun or at least make their lives even more hell than it already is.

62361602
Musk proved that markets are not efficient and humanity is retarded. Just like how the 20th century proved socialism is retarded, the 21st century will prove capitalism is retarded and have the world will go socialist allowing the 22nd century to once again prove socialism to be retarded.

item.title: He's now worth $1 trillion, approximately the GDP of Poland. — 27 replies — 27.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
He's now worth $1 trillion, approximately the GDP of Poland.

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-06-13 00:57 UTC

OP

He's now worth $1 trillion, approximately the GDP of Poland. Do you agree that he needs to start punishing his detractors directly? Someone this rich should not be insulted any more than the kings of the past.

Early replies (first 3)

62355366
Completely meaningless comparison. He can't cash out that trillion. And GDP can be manipulated and defined in many ways

62355372
>>62355222
Net worth isn't influence. Elon is the global equivalent of a redneck lottery winner. No one takes him seriously. He couldn't even last 6 months as a White House advisor because he was so stupid he hired nothing but 20 year old broccoli head zoomers.

62355379
elon musk is gayyyyyyy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355381
>>62355372
>Elon is the global equivalent of a redneck lottery winner. No one takes him seriously

Back to r***it, loser

62355390
>>62355381
Why did he get kicked out of the leader of the free world's cabinet then? Jews or some other inane bullshit? Or because he couldn't deliver on the easiest lay-up in history (reducing waste in the US government)?

62355406
>>62355390
He's too honest (autism) for neurotypicals who feel slighted. He has no talent for political scheming

62355408
>>62355406
in other words, that anon is right and elon is retarded

62355415
>>62355408
Your samefagging isn't subtle at all

62355422
>>62355222
>GDP
Why are you comparing the monetary turnover in a country with the market value of a company?
The market value of the land in Poland alone is probably 25 trillion $+.

62355426
>>62355390
Do you actually think it's easy to get the Government to agree to cutting waste?

62355462
>>62355426
I think it would help to go after actual waste and not hare brained bullshit like the National Weather Service and air traffic controllers. He got a bunch of kids to ctrl+f buzzwords in goverment documents and kill anything that mentioned them. Once the US started running into problems running basic infrastructure because of le reddit man they fired him.

62355530
>>62355415
I'll differentfag him then, he is correct. I wouldn't voote for any of these two fake parties though.

62355597
>>62355406
>he's too honest
LMAO the guy would make a horrible poker player. He's constantly telegraphing that he's lying in interviews.

62355610
>>62355390
Special government employees can only serve 13 days you fucking retard

62355623
>>62355610
*130

62355628
>>62355372
wait i thought money was free speech. so by legal metric, elon has MORE free speech than you do.

62355666
>>62355381
Elon worship is r*ddit bevavior

62355678
>>62355222
THIS IS NOT RIGHT
HE COULD GIVE EVERYONE ON EARTH $1 MILLION AND END WORLD POVERTY ONE MAN DOES NOT NEED THAT MUCH MONEY!

62360803
>>62355530
Then you have no influence at all. Even Ross Perot only got 18% of votes despite being the best candidate

62360822
>>62355372
>He couldn't even last 6 months as a White House advisor because he was so stupid he hired nothing but 20 year old broccoli head zoomers.
He may be dumb at times, but people like you are usually even more dumb. You don't even know why what you said is stupid

62360828
>>62355666
Lmao all of Reddit hates him wtf are you talking about

You think the commies on Reddit like the richest person in the world wtf are you smoking

62360847
>>62355222
I agree. A show about his detractors and how pathetic their miserable existence is in comparison to the Greatest Man in the world would make for lovely entertainment.

62360848
Impeaach the orange fraud with someone who deals with business and taxes on a yearly basis, like bezos or zuckerberg. They would be infinitely better in understanding economics.

This orange nigger has only destroyed stocks, caused war, caused shit to go up in price and set open a whole new can of worms geopolitically.

62360850
He cannot dump 1 trillion worth of shares so he does not own 1 trillion. It's like when Saylor becomes a trillionaire. It's not really liquid, but it looks cool.

62360903
redditniggers in absolute shambles, i can almost taste their seethe from behind my screen

62360911
>>62355222
he's only worth that much on paper though

62361585
>>62355222
As much as I would like that, I think even kings saw some entertaining value of a punching bag that can't shut up about them knowing that their life is wrong and full of cope coming out of their mouth.

item.title: XRP **EMERGENCY** UPDATE — 14 replies — 20.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP **EMERGENCY** UPDATE

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-13 01:14 UTC

OP

Some breaking xrp news

Early replies (first 3)

62351536
Remember when Brad said he didnt want to support the clarity act?

62351601
>>62351406
Why do you keep making these corny linkie threads to cope with objectively losing?
>>62351536
He literally never said that. He said he would support a bill which served crypto and would oppose one which didn't in the very early days of the formulation when the Clarity Act was leaning a bad direction. He has literally posted to mark up and pass it repeatedly and been lobbying for its passage on mainstream news on Fox Business. Why would you choose to be so low IQ and frustrated rather than change your thesis based on what's true? INSANE cope.

62354640
>>62351406
KABOOOOOOOOOOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355362
>>62351406
So bullish

62355367
>>62351406
Did we get our assets handed to us?

62355473
>>62351601
Why do you keep posting this chart as some sort of gotcha when it shows you're down 70% from ATH?

62356152
>>62355473
Because it demonstrates my point completely and I know it makes you mad and that you are bad at coping with the madness it causes. The entire broad market is crushed and still XRP in the most recent change of guard is crushing everything else and over 100% up. Why do you stay in positions you know have no real thesis behind them to cope instead of changing based on what's true? My dream is that the entire market crashes 90% more to buy one last time providing nothing essential caused it outside valuation washouts as has been the case because the crypto I invest in has an actual purpose and the crypto you invest in has only marketing and is literally designed to get you to buy to fund corporate ops.

62356157
>>62351406
Are those the chainlink colours? Very bullish

62356164
>>62356152
This post triggered me so I’m giving you a (you). Just know you’re one of the dumbest niggers around if you actually believe what you posted.

62358620
>>62356164
You are as a community down 70% on average on the position you hold and 90% from ATH 6 years ago despite only launching a few years before that. In the new crypto paradigm post-Chokepoint with ETH no longer being carried by lawfare where it cannot go on tech, LINK is down more against HBAR and XLM and XRP than the most legendary stock traders in history made in their careers investing and link is down in proper terms vs. the dollar. Cope.

62359134
So bullish for the ripple xrp

62359952
>>62358620
You are literally down 70% from ATH delusional xrpaypig

62360140
Is it, dare I say, time to play ball?

62361623
>>62359952
I am literally up more than 5x on my XRP position. You are LITERALLY down 70-80 on your link one as a community average.

item.title: We need to talk about this — 11 replies — 12.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
We need to talk about this

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-13 01:01 UTC

OP

I made a thread earlier today about MrBeast doing a livestream to rub everyone's face into his recently 500M subs. The livestream has a live chat enable where people spam their flags. To my surprise, only shitskin flags were spammed: India (by a lot), Bangladesh, Nepal, Nigeria, etc. No western flag. On that thread I made a comment about being it the reason for his sub room temperature IQ content being so popular. After all, shitskins outnumber Whites 10 to 1. In that thread, a anon made a comment that is a direct consequence of the browning of the Internet since the mid 2010s.

I've pondered on this fact, and in the light of the current ridiculous valuation of SpaceX, it all made sense to me. For those Millennial boomers like me and older, have you notice how since the mid 2010s, market valuation everywhere started NOT to make sense anymore? Bitcoin, prediction markets, overvalued hyped meme stocks, P/E over fucking 100 and the like. That's the fucking reason. Niggers, jeets, chinks and beaners using international aid money in order to play Gordon Gekko on the markets. Internet access practically turns your gooning room into a trading floor the like that would make a 1980s trader envious. With such low IQ brown goyim flooding the Internet, it wasn't long since everything went to shit online. That's also why any decent looking White girl with round firm knockers can make a killing on OF. She's pocketing millions of thirsty simping streetshitters. Bobs and vagene as we say.

When you think about it, the government uses taxes to collect your pay check, then gives that hard earned money to kikes in pissrael for "defence" and to caca-colored mongroids as international aid. Then the latter uses the money to enrich tech jews and terminally online sluts. There's literally a pipeline from the pockets of White men, to the pockets of the most wealthy jews out there, without you having any say on it.

Early replies (first 3)

62360091
>>62360063
They call him

MISTER BEAST

for a reason.

62360117
>>62360063
The Chinese firewall that we used to make fun of was actually high IQ.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62360142
so you're saying i shouldn't bet against indians and africans being retarded and should double down on whatever retard investments excite the browns? how will i know ahead of time what excites them so i can get in early?

62360147
>>62360117
the north korean internet is even higher IQ - you get access to administrative service portal, educational resources and something like curated wikipedia.. plus the propaganda stuff.. but there is no social media, so ITS GOLD

62360161
>>62360063

62360165
>>62360074
It's not schizo. I'm right about this 100%, and it is becoming a widespread socioeconomic problem

62361487
>>62360063
Ok so say theoretically we lose 20% of indians and indian like people due to starvation and an energy crisis, what happens to valuations and only fans whores then?

62361517
>>62360063
The US needs a firewall that blocks all foreign nations outside of the West and Japan. The problem is that so many of these shitskins have migrated to the West in the last 10-20 years. 80% of leaf posters are fucking jeets at this point

62361529
>>62360063
>For those Millennial boomers like me and older, have you notice how since the mid 2010s,
it's also extremely hard to find any project that actually goes up like bitcoin since 2021. Shit is fucked because of brown scams.

62361597
>>62360142
Buy a stock. Wait for the fish to bite. Look to see if institutional investors are there yet first. They bite then they make it a meme stock. I call it whaling. Also look for anything odd that will make it an organic meme stock.

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 12 replies — 10.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-13 00:44 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361478
>>62361471
>He hasn't even done anything, period.
he's made more progress in electric vehicles, private space industry, bionics, robotics, and ai than anyone from the previous couple million years.

And pretending he didn't do it because he paid other people is ignoring the fact that millions of people could have paid other people to invent stuff and didn't.

62361481
>>62361452
Because investing, as suggest by the name is about speculating on future performance. And a company that reliably goes to space has a potentially bright future. Now stop making threads and go read an ai summary of basic investing concepts you midwit reddit faggot.

62361499
>>62361452
>companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted
But it is now the future, and they are performing poorly, the BYD makes superior electric vehicles, now with Sodium batteries that charge in minutes and don't pose a fire risk. The only thing Tesla has going for it is Countries like America, and Germany not allowing Chinese vehicles for import.

62361515
>>62361499
turns out nobody likes chinkshit and betting against the US economy gets you annihilated, who knew?

62361539
>>62361459
>>62361481
these are bots

62361541
stop asking questions

62361542
keep seething

62361548
>>62361515
ive noticed every single post starting with
>turns out
and
>its almost like
are always the gayest most reddit tier gorilla nigger retard takes

62361549
>>62361539

item.title: Law of the jungle — 52 replies — 140.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-06-13 01:33 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387
>nigger tier envy thread
>/biz/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62360745
>>62360694
you're an employee?
>>62360713
>>62360717
how much do you make a year?

62360749
>>62359336
I think there will be opportunities either with businesses in the city or at rich estates in the country as hired guns. Informal security that is marginally cheaper than experienced pros. This as well as construction associated with hardening structures against intrusion, assault and vandalism. All muh police people in this thread should look at police response times in Flint and similar spiraling shitholes, because this is about to be standard nationwide. The only way to be competitive is low overhead otherwise I think I could pay dickheads to stand around with maverick 88s and police trade in glocks.

62360752
>>62360745
how poor are you anon? you sound very poor.

62360783
>>62360752
i am an employee. i only make $153k a year.

62360788
>>62360745
I am at 215k annually and have over 2 million invested in various companies

62360790
Damn why are you poors so angry at someone else being successful, stop being so dumb and lazy

62360793
>>62360788
so you're poor -- otherwise you wouldn't be working. people with money aren't employees. and you defend this?

62360809
>>62360749
Damn I just looked into this and its much easier and cheaper than I envisioned. Anons, focus on your shitcoins and blow off top tech equities and your comfy wfh jobs totally safe from AI. I'm unironically going to be building my empire.

62360896
>>62360793
It is called building generational wealth. I don't just want to secure my future, I want to secure the futures of my grandchildren and great grandchildren. You wouldn't understand because your brain is too tiny

62360928
Anyone got that video of the CEO who got stabbed to death by a homeless man at 3AM? That drunk asshole felt invincible.

62360943
>>62359336
Ah yes.
Ever heard of this little thing called the Ten Commandments?
Hmmm yes.
Monkeys like you are reverting to pre-civilization.

62360977
>>62359336
If wealth isn't important why kill for it? Is this a personal vendetta or something?

62360991
it's interesting that rich defenders don't bring up a social contract
because in the west that's long gone

62361033
>>62360991
The most important social contract is the Ten Commandments.
It's the commies and anarchist left that does away with them.

62361047
>>62360991
> rich defenders
Wtf is that even? Since when do intelligence and/or success deserve to be seen as punishable negatives? What's there to defend about being rich? Are you insane?

62361048
>>62360896
generational wealth? you make $200k and a $2 million net worth. lmao.

62361065
>>62360991
literally just pay him to chill. its that easy

62361074
>>62359496
Those are airsoft shit.

62361079
>>62360200
>kekaroo
This gay aussie /pol/ faggot spams /biz/
Lovely.

62361121
get a trillion dollars and find out what happens
oh, nothing happens? ok

62361214
I know a god will hunting troll when I see one.

62361399
>>62359496
These are BB guns, brownoid.
Google what that is, since you don't know.

62361400
>>62359336
Based

62361423
>>62361048
2 mil in stocks/crypto not 2 mil net worth and unlike you I don't need to cry and piss my pants over rich people

62361436
kinda rude

62361646
>>62361423
plywood box equity doesn't count. and you aren't rich, because you work for a living. literally no one in the history of mankind has created generational worth from working. you need to own a business, which you don't.

62361649
>>62361646
if he has that much in stocks and crypto he probably owns a business.

all you have to do is buy and sell in a single fiscal year and you're technically in business.

62361653
>>62359336
Nigger, you will never do shit, you never have done shit. Your impotent crying school shooter fantasy will not improve you situation, while your mother is getting rutted by the tenth nigger of the day

62361673
>>62360694
kek poorfag and a stinkoid brownoid baggie. i am so much better than you its unreal

62361674
>>62361653
>nigger cuck bbc fantasy posting while defending trillionaire jews online
i am whiter than you

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 82 replies — 52.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 82 • Updated: 2026-06-13 00:21 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62344811
>>62342770
How poorfag coded are you people that you think just maxing out a couple of credit cards is a lot of money? Like 10-20k might sound like " a lot of money", but even if you aren't rich thats like a year or two of savings, how is that shit going to last you your entire life?

62344833
You won't make it as a blue collar wagie but that doesn't mean its a dead end

Open your own business and build, in the end youre the boss of blue collar wagies and make more than 100k, blue collar work is high in demand and always will, someone has to build the physical shit and it aont be KI

62346901
>>62342700
never change, anon. I enjoy your little gay larps. i'm your fan, you're funny as fuck, I wish we had a biz party somewhere, you'd be the soul of the party unironically speaking

62346960
>>62346901
What larp?

62346974
>>62344611
>trust fund leeches.
Kek. Seething working class.
>>62344811
> How poorfag coded are you people that you think just maxing out a couple of credit cards is a lot of money
Not poor but maxing out one credit card is a lot of money.

62349408
>>62342798
bro you're onto something.
Fake accounts FLOOD the internet. Especially kinky shit like fetlife and the kink subreddits - they will have the exact same contact for totally different account and profiles.
I found 2 accounts that had each other as friends.. they had similar profiles, said they "knew each other online" and 1 used a keyword that the other one was talking to me and using..
there is a MASSIVE psyop happening.

62349422
>>62342700
OP, you clearly haven't heard this enough, you're a faggot.

62351731
>>62346960
everything that you post (on an almost daily basis) that never actually happened outside your autistic mongoloid mind, champ. I love these stories about the "middle class" lol. Keep up the good work soldier. thanks for the laughs (unironically speaking)

62354799
>>62342905
Elliot only drove a BMW because his dad had some cushy Hollywood job working on films like Hunger Games, etc. Pretty sure he wrote something in his manifesto seething about seeing some guy driving a shitbox and still having a beautiful girl in the passenger seat too.

62354802
>>62342834
ha, i was thinking this read exactly like that post

62354821
>>62342828
When they're writing long form fiction they get a pass.

62355262
>>62342700
Electrical contractor here. I live closer to the beach than you do. It’s kind of funny, most skilled tradesmen look down on people like yourself. We feel sorry for you because in the grand scheme of things - you’re worthless. Unable to build or fix anything. Totally dependent on others. Kinda pathetic

62355284
>>62355262
The mind of a blue collar is truly pathetic.

62355301
>>62342770
I haven’t worked in 1,5 months and i can already feel myself wanting to just become passive and not really being impressed or motivated by anything (brain getting fried by dopamine and serotonine). You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.

62355307
>>62355301
You’re pathetic. Since a little kid, all the classmates of my have been retired.

62355313
>>62355301
> You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
You’re probably just poor. People making a top 10% income from investments tend not to work.

62355322
>>62355301
>1.5 months
You don’t even have a non working lifestyle built yet.

62355326
>>62355307
This may be the funniest claim I've seen you make. Absolutely nobody from your school does any kind of work at all? Most people do find things to keep themselves occupied with, beyond mere shitposting on an anonymous uzbekistani finger painting notification board.

62355338
>>62355326
Maybe we grew up in different classes.
On one hand, /biz/ loves to larp as rich and successful and on the other hand, loves to larp as muh productive and I don’t like doing nothing.
Fucking jokes.
I hear people say what you say all the time. Yet look at retired people, I don’t see you saying shit to them. Yet when it is someone young and not working, suddenly they are “unproductive.” Even though these people are clearly rich.

62355342
>>62355307
>>62355313
>>62355322
Kek did i trigger you too much?

62355360
>>62355342
I have never in my life looked at someone not working and thought anything bad.
Growing up, my dad retired when I was like 5. By 10, the parents of all my school friends were retired long ago.
By past college, the differences between the haves and have nots became noticeable because college is where you truly see class differences because high school and under tend to mainly kids from your neighborhood.
You cannot imagine life without work yet kids that grew up like me were surrounded by adults that don’t work. It is the normal. People like me just carry on the lifestyle.
People from successful families don’t break their back.

62355375
>>62355301
Depends what you mean by work. But any occupation today is not a good use of time. Like work as in what? Something you need to get a college degree for? White collar?
Or something blue collar? That’s even dumber.
Starting your own business? Okay sure, if you want to start it as a hobby..?
I don’t know. People build lifestyles when they don’t need to work to live.
Some travel, some have many social gatherings
>Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
Oh yeah, the bottom 90% definitely feel alive struggling lol.
Struggling for what? Lmao.

62355404
>>62355301
Sometimes I see the kids in my neighborhood. This isnt an ultra rich 100m a year neighborhood or millions a month neighborhood but in 10-20 years when these kids grow up, it would be a waste of time for them to get a “job.”
Get it?

62357658
>>62355301
Rabbis rabbiposting as usual. Nice try but no, i won't work for (((you)))

62359024
>>62342770
I have 300k...where should i buy a house and get on gibbs? Caliwali?

62359462
>>62342700
I mean someone has to like do stuff u need someone to actually do stuff

62359491
>>62342770
This is called capitalism btw. You either exploit others or u work until you die. Hello knock knock HELLO this is basic capitalism. Wake up everything u guys complain about is due to market forces the Jews are a fantasy of cohesion to keep your little world simple bigga

62360989
Never worked a day in my life and I would never shit on a trade they keep my water running and the electricity flowing. Would you trust a 3rd worlder to wire up your outlet in your house and not expect to die in your sleep when that shit catches fire fuck no I'm going to pay a white American 150$ an hour to do that shit right. Without their goods and services our money doesn't mean shit and I might aswell get raped by a pack of niggers.

62361159
All work is automateable. No one of you should feel safe.

62361494
>>62344611
>the producing members
The secret is these types wouldnt know what to do in life other than work. Their life centers around it. You're supposed to exploit this in them

item.title: You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn — 76 replies — 41.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-06-13 00:05 UTC

OP

wage

Early replies (first 3)

62351330
I'm currently hiding in the bathroom

62351353
>>62351325
Im not desu

62351358
>>62351325
i hate being a wagie so fucking much

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62354661
>>62354608
one of the trick is to live very near your workplace, im lucky as my apartment is only 10 mins max of travel, sometimes i go home and eat on my lunch breaks

this is a bit luck based, but a job where you can slack off if your main shit is done. my boss dont care what I do desu as long as his online pickup stats are good so sometimes I do my shit during work time

other jobs i can think of is something like subway where theres not much to do during downtime and you're often lowly staffed like 2-3 people max so its very relaxed. i dont think you could chill during downtime in something like mcdonalds for example

8 hours of sleep/8 hours of work and usually around 6 hours of free time for me after showering cooking etc

62354675
>>62354655
dam my condolences

62354681
I'm sitting on the toilet now while waging.

I always take multiple shits per day while being paid for it.

62354699
>>62354675
It’s such a joke. Trucking is a nightmare, no wonder jeets do it

62354715
>>62352824
I can't or I get in biiiiiiig trouble

62354716
>>62351325
I being paid to sleep in a hotel because tradies keep fucking up the infrastructure for our machine

62354761
>>62354699
Can't believe how many jets have jumped into the industry. I feel like the average normie isn't aware of their presence in that sector.

62354806
>>62354761
People have no idea how much danger they are in on the highway because of the shitjeets. It’s an existential threats to everybody

62354992
>>62354661
Idk maybe I'm weak but I need to "decompress" after work

62355007
>>62354992
> Idk maybe I'm weak but I need to "decompress" after work
Same. It is so draining.

62356088
I have alot of down time at my workplace too
big issue for me is a 2 hour commute every day but I put up with it since my rent is cheap

62356156
>>62353364
Ash Grove. In that pic I was troubleshooting a truck loading issue. I discovered that our loading system was haunting loading due to some shutdown condition for a split second and tgen deciding to be happy again, most likely fluctuations in the scale wait from the truck or scale shaking while loading. The momentary blip breaks a logic latch forcing a retard of the loading process each time. Now I get to find out how to use timer off delays in unity pro and proworx32 to filter out that momentary blip. Every day is a new problem I have to figure out and I enjoy that.

62356177
>>62356156
I like how my phone knows that I call people retards on 4chan so often it autocorrect a typo of reload to retard

62356574
I'm shitting

62356712
I have an HR adult daycare job. I’m busy for maybe 40 hours a month. Most of my free time is spent shitposting here or reading the news. Today the only thing I have going on is a 1 hour meeting in the afternoon.
>>62356088
I just got my MBA, mostly paid for by the job. I used pretty much all the downtime I typically had to do coursework at work. Shit was so cash.

62356735
I'm taking a break and sitting on a bench at the Bass Pro Shop museum in Dania Beach

62356749
I'm wfh, haven't done anything all afternoon except shitpost on /biz/ and do some laundry

62356784
I'm trying to hire a line cook but nobody wants to work. Need to find a make work adult daycare job.

62357448
>>62356712
How do you get a bullshit HR job if you are a dude? Are you brown?

62357470
>>62351325
I hope we all make it and never have to do this shit again

62357873
I'm at the airport lounge enjoying booze and snacks. Today is remote work day, just finished a teams call, now I just need to wrap up the week by spending about a minute to update one confluence item.

62358052
>>62351325
Nah I live in Europe so I'm done for the week. 100% WFH cybersecurity. Shit's so cash.

62358146
>>62357448
I’m White.
I got lucky and was hired by the HR department’s budget manager, who is also a white male.
My role is more that of an accountant than what you would think of as an HR role, but in the company registry I’m technically HR. My boss (that same budget manager) and I handle the HR department’s finances and try to keep the roasties on budget. They’re pretty deferential, and my boss is very hands on, so that doesn’t leave me with a lot to do beyond monthly reconciliation reports and paying some bills as they come up.
It’s a sweet gig, the only shit part is that it pays peanuts.

62358716
>>62356712
>Today the only thing I have going on is a 1 hour meeting in the afternoon
And they just canceled it, lel. My weekend is starting early.

62359402
>>62351325
I'm in class, but I'm not paying attention to it.

62359670
>>62351325
wrong image OP

WAGE

62360176
>>62351868
I want to visit Thailand.

62360948
>>62356735
Holy comfy

62360968
>>62354378
How?

62361451
I legitimately don't think about my job at work 80% of the time as I'm usually busy browsing /biz/, playing online Catan or talking to Claude while "working". The other 20% is me trying to figure out how I asking out my coworker so obviously likes me, but without the chance of having an awkward conversation with HR before my termination.

item.title: My dad is dumping his life savings into the SPCX IPO — 26 replies — 19.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
My dad is dumping his life savings into the SPCX IPO

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-13 00:33 UTC

OP

I'm not going to get an inheritance, am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62356221
Well, NO!

62356238
>>62356203
Calm down faggot, it's a Musk company, line goes up

62356256
>>62356238
EVERYBODY IS SAYING THAT KEK.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62357148
>>62356203
by monday you'll be a billionaire. you made it anon.

62357258
>>62356238
Tesla is currently at about the same price it was in 2021

62357370
>>62356203
Either way you're not getting anything, either he loses it all or spends it all on SEA sex tours.

62357388
>>62356203
Jews deserve it more than you

62357430
>>62356203
Do you know how much raw fuel and the ungodly amount of rare metals we can extract in the moon? That's the value of SPCX and your dad's money just landed there.

62357459
>>62356203
hope he wants to hold it for 20 years because there won't be any real space "exploration" for a long while from SPCX

62357750
>>62356203
Maybe tell your dad to buy BTC, those that did some years back are freaking rich now. I save up monthly on BTC for my son, I stake like 10% too on babylon, in 10 years he will thank me

62357910
>>62357370
My father did this

62360902
>>62357370
Accurate. Unfortunately.

62360963
>>62356203
>My dad is dumping his life savings into the SPCX IPO

IF he got in at $135, then AWESOME

62361088
>>62360963
This is not how you post objects on /biz/!

62361111
>>62361088
correct and checked

62361162
>>62357148
and by Tuesday? broke

62361351
>>62361111
It is literally the guy who posts pictures of his gold coins on PMG. Every thread has a picture of this sort of thing on top of his fantasy pirate treasure map. I had to make a biz version of pic above.

62361363
>>62356203
it's not gonna go to 0
you might get 1-2 cents on the dollar

62361408
>>62361351
>PMG. Every thread has a picture of this sort of thing on top of his fantasy pirate treasure map

kek, need to visit PMG way more often.

62361414
>>62356203
most people don't get an inheritance. Let it go. Maybe dream of winning the lottery or finding buried pirate treasure.

62361418
>>62361363
I owned stock in something that was worth less than 1 cent per share by the time I noticed the dump. I was trying to sell to get what was left of my money for months. But Fidelity wouldn't let me for some bullshit reason. Finally worked at the start of this month. I could have got out 4-6 cents instead of 1 with my 12 shares. Oh no it is not close enough to stock value you need a better exit plan. This is my exit plan ass wipe! I am pulling out for what written on the ticker! NOW GET THIS DISTRACTING SHIT OFF MY PORTFOLIO!!!

62361434
>>62357258
Okay and where is Tesla compared to its IPO?

62361442
>>62356203
enjoy your generational wealth. lucky bastard

62361458
>>62361418
those cents could generate money for you rather than just sit around
it compounds

62361530
>>62356203
I told my parents to put as much money as they could into it, and they ignored me. You're lucky.
